text.title: Task Splitting Strategies and WBS
text.summary: When to split tasks, three splitting strategies (by component, by layer, by dependency wave), WBS principles, and the wave-based task creation pattern.

rule.statement: Split a task if: context estimate exceeds 80k tokens, task touches more than 10 files, task requires deep research across many modules, task has multiple independent sub-goals. Calculate budget BEFORE writing the TASK file.
rule.rationale: Tasks over 80k tokens are at risk of context overflow. Estimates that reveal a 120k task should trigger a split before any TASK file is created — not after.

section.title: Splitting Strategy: By Component
section.p: Split one large task into smaller tasks, each responsible for one component.

example.code:
Before: TASK-001: Implement user authentication (150k tokens)
After: TASK-001: Create user database schema (25k tokens) TASK-002: Implement password hashing utilities (20k tokens) TASK-003: Create registration handler (30k tokens) TASK-004: Create login handler (35k tokens) TASK-005: Add authentication middleware (25k tokens)
example.note: First sub-task has zero dependencies — Wave 1 can start immediately.

section.title: Splitting Strategy: By Layer
section.p: Split one large task into horizontal layer tasks (data, service, API, UI).

example.code:
Before: TASK-001: Build payment system (180k tokens)
After: TASK-001: Payment database models (30k tokens) TASK-002: Payment service layer (40k tokens) TASK-003: Payment API endpoints (35k tokens) TASK-004: Payment UI components (35k tokens) TASK-005: Payment integration tests (40k tokens)

section.title: Splitting Strategy: By Dependency Wave
section.p: Split one large task into wave-gated sub-tasks where later waves use patterns from earlier waves.

example.code:
Before: TASK-001: Implement notification system (140k tokens)
After: Wave 1 (parallel): TASK-001: Notification data models (25k tokens) TASK-002: Email sender utility (20k tokens)
Wave 2 (depends on Wave 1): TASK-003: Notification service (35k tokens) TASK-004: Notification templates (25k tokens)
Wave 3 (depends on Wave 2): TASK-005: Notification API (35k tokens)

rule.statement: Apply three WBS rules: 100% Rule (all work is accounted for, no orphaned work), Mutually Exclusive (no overlap between tasks), Completeness (each task has clear done criteria). Test-writing tasks and CHANGELOG updates must appear explicitly.
rule.rationale: WBS omissions are the most common source of "surprise" work at the end of a feature. Test writing and documentation are consistently omitted when the plan focuses on production code only.

rule.statement: Create detailed TASK_*.md files in waves, not all at once. Create and execute Wave 1 first; then create Wave 2 files using patterns learned from Wave 1 execution reports.
rule.rationale: Task files created before Wave 1 execution are based on design assumptions that may be wrong. Wave 2 files created after Wave 1 reflect actual implementation patterns, reducing rework.

antipattern.description: Agents asked to "create all tasks now" create Wave 2 and Wave 3 stubs with placeholder content.
antipattern.reason: Placeholder task files degrade the quality of Wave 2 and 3 execution. Enforce wave-gated creation in the planning prompt.
